# Data Model: Hamburger Menu with Favourites

**Feature**: 004-hamburger-favourites  
**Date**: 2026-05-26

---

## New Application State

### FAVOURITES_STORE *(new)*

Server-side in-memory dict in `src/app.py`. Keyed by user UUID; value is a list of paper dicts in reverse chronological order (most recently added first).

```python
FAVOURITES_STORE: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}
# Key:   user UUID (str) — generated by secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
# Value: list[dict] — each dict is a serialised PaperArticle, newest first
```

**Operations**:

| Operation | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| Add favourite | `FAVOURITES_STORE[user_id].insert(0, paper_dict)` — prepend for newest-first ordering |
| Remove favourite | Filter out by `paper_dict['id'] == item_id` |
| List favourites | `FAVOURITES_STORE.get(user_id, [])` — already in display order |
| Check membership | `any(f['id'] == item_id for f in FAVOURITES_STORE.get(user_id, []))` |

**Deduplication invariant**: Before adding, check membership. If already present, remove instead (toggle).

---

### Session Cookie *(minimal extension)*

Flask signed-cookie session, extended with one new key:

| Key | Type | Description |
|-----|------|-------------|
| `user_id` | `str` | URL-safe 32-byte random token generated on first visit via `secrets.token_urlsafe(32)`. Used as the key into `FAVOURITES_STORE`. |

Cookie size impact: `user_id` adds ~45 bytes to the existing session cookie, well within the 4KB browser limit.

---

## Paper Record (stored in FAVOURITES_STORE)

Each favourite stores a plain dict with all fields required to render the full detail view without re-fetching from arXiv.

| Field | Type | Source | Required for |
|-------|------|--------|-------------|
| `id` | `str` (URL) | PaperArticle.id | Unique key, detail route |
| `title` | `str` | PaperArticle.title | Favourites list, detail page |
| `authors` | `list[str]` | PaperArticle.authors | Favourites list, detail page |
| `summary` | `str` | PaperArticle.summary | Detail page (FR-011 fallback) |
| `url` | `str` | PaperArticle.url | Detail page ("Open original source" link) |
| `published_at` | `str` (ISO 8601) | PaperArticle.published_at | Favourites list, detail page |
| `source_label` | `str` | PaperArticle.source_label | Detail page meta line |
| `fetched_at` | `str` (ISO 8601) | PaperArticle.fetched_at | Metadata completeness |
| `metadata` | `dict[str, str]` | PaperArticle.metadata | Metadata completeness |

This is the same dict format produced by `PaperArticle.to_dict()` — no new serialisation logic required.

---

## Existing Models (unchanged)

- **`PaperArticle`** (`src/models/article.py`) — no structural changes; `to_dict()` output used directly as the stored favourite record
- **`LATEST_RESULTS`** — unchanged; still `{"arxiv": [list[dict]]}`

---

## State Transitions

### Favourite toggle flow

```
User on /detail/{item_id}
    └─► Clicks heart button (♡ or ♥)
            └─► POST /favourite/toggle  {item_id}
                    ├─► paper already in FAVOURITES_STORE[user_id]?
                    │       YES → remove (filter out by id)
                    │       NO  → look up in LATEST_RESULTS["arxiv"]
                    │               → prepend to FAVOURITES_STORE[user_id]
                    └─► 302 Redirect → GET /detail/{item_id}
                                └─► is_favourite = True/False passed to template
                                        └─► Heart renders ♥ or ♡
```

### Remove from Favourites page

```
User on /favourites
    └─► Clicks × next to a paper
            └─► POST /favourite/remove  {item_id}
                    └─► filter FAVOURITES_STORE[user_id] to exclude item_id
                            └─► 302 Redirect → GET /favourites
                                    └─► updated list rendered
```

### Detail view fallback (FR-011)

```
GET /detail/{item_id}
    └─► Search LATEST_RESULTS["arxiv"] by id
            ├─► Found → render detail.html
            └─► Not found → search FAVOURITES_STORE[user_id] by id
                    ├─► Found → render detail.html (from stored favourite data)
                    └─► Not found → abort(404)
```

---

## Uniqueness & Ordering Invariants

- **No duplicates**: `FAVOURITES_STORE[user_id]` never contains two entries with the same `id`. Enforced on every add by the toggle logic.
- **Reverse chronological order**: New papers are prepended (`insert(0, ...)`). The list is always ready to render without sorting.
- **User isolation**: Each `user_id` key in `FAVOURITES_STORE` is isolated; one user's favourites cannot affect another's.
